title: Uncovering multidimensional effects of generative AI on learning
excerpt: A research team consisting of researcher Somi Joo, Professor Changjun Lee and Professor Daeho Lee from the Sungkyunkwan University (SKKU) Department of Artificial Intelligence Convergence have published a study comprehensively analyzing the opportunities and risks of using generative artificial intelligence (GAI) in educational settings. The study examines how students have recently used generative AI in education.
content_html:
A research team consisting of researcher Somi Joo, Professor Changjun Lee and Professor Daeho Lee from the Sungkyunkwan University (SKKU) Department of Artificial Intelligence Convergence have published a study comprehensively analyzing the opportunities and risks of using generative artificial intelligence (GAI) in educational settings. The study examines how students have recently used generative AI in education.

The research team conducted an experiment involving 88 university students, using evaluation questions equivalent to the domestic high school academic achievement level. The learning process was divided into three stages: "Concept Understanding," to read and comprehend new content; "Problem Solving," to solve problems based on what was learned; and "Result Review," to check one's own answers. Students were then randomly assigned to use generative AI (GPT-4o) at different stages to track its effects.

The experimental results showed that the group that directly solved problems using generative AI recorded higher scores on a subsequent test than the group that used AI only during the concept-understanding stage. This was because the hints and core summaries provided by the AI reduced excessive cognitive stress and unnecessary processes during problem-solving, drastically increasing learning efficiency.

However, an interesting twist emerged as dependency on generative AI increased. Students who usually relied heavily on AI tended to have lower actual test scores within the problem-solving group. They accepted answers provided by the AI uncritically, skipping the essential process of deeply contemplating and making the knowledge their own.

On the other hand, although these highly dependent students experienced a drop in actual scores, their "perceived learning"—the feeling that they studied well—and their "learning satisfaction" were much higher. This is interpreted as a result of the AI organizing complex content in an easy-to-read manner and providing clear answers, inducing a sort of learning illusion that makes students feel as if they have perfectly understood everything on their own.

Professor Changjun Lee of SKKU emphasized, "Generative AI is a powerful tool that increases learning efficiency, but relying on it uncritically can lead to the paradoxical result of lowering objective academic achievement."

He added, "An educational guideline is urgently needed to foster students' independent questioning skills and critical verification abilities so that generative AI can function properly as a learning aid rather than a substitute."

This study is evaluated to have presented a specific direction on 'how to use it properly,' rather than the unconditional regulation or blind adoption of generative AI in the education sector.

Who's behind this story?

Sadie Harley

BSc Life Sciences & Ecology. Microbiology lab background with pharmaceutical news experience in oil, gas, and renewable industries. Full profile →

Andrew Zinin

Master's in physics with research experience. Long-time science news enthusiast. Plays key role in Science X's editorial success. Full profile →

Citation: Uncovering multidimensional effects of generative AI on learning (2026, July 21) retrieved 22 July 2026 from https://phys.org/news/2026-07-uncovering-multidimensional-effects-generative-ai.html

content_html_zh:
成均馆大学 (SKKU) 人工智能融合系的研究员 Somi Joo、Changjun Lee 教授和 Daeho Lee 教授组成的研究小组发表了一项研究，全面分析了在教育环境中使用生成人工智能 (GAI) 的机会和风险。 该研究调查了学生最近如何在教育中使用生成式人工智能。

研究团队对88名大学生进行了实验，使用相当于国内高中学业成绩水平的评价问题。 学习过程分为三个阶段：“概念理解”，阅读并理解新内容； “解决问题”，根据所学知识解决问题；和“结果审核”，检查自己的答案。 然后，学生被随机分配在不同阶段使用生成式人工智能（GPT-4o）来跟踪其效果。

实验结果表明，直接使用生成式人工智能解决问题的小组在随后的测试中比仅在概念理解阶段使用人工智能的小组取得了更高的分数。 这是因为人工智能提供的提示和核心总结减少了解决问题时过度的认知压力和不必要的过程，大大提高了学习效率。

然而，随着对生成式人工智能的依赖增加，出现了一个有趣的转折。 通常严重依赖人工智能的学生在问题解决组中的实际测试分数往往较低。 他们不加批判地接受人工智能提供的答案，跳过了深入思考和将知识转化为己有的基本过程。

另一方面，虽然这些高度依赖的学生的实际成绩有所下降，但他们的“感知学习”——他们学习得很好的感觉——和他们的“学习满意度”要高得多。 这被解释为人工智能以易于阅读的方式组织复杂的内容并提供清晰的答案的结果，引发了一种学习错觉，让学生感觉好像他们自己完全理解了一切。

SKKU教授Changjun Lee强调：“生成式人工智能是提高学习效率的强大工具，但不加批判地依赖它可能会导致降低客观学术成就的矛盾结果。”

他补充说：“迫切需要制定教育指南来培养学生的独立提问技能和批判性验证能力，以便生成式人工智能能够作为学习辅助工具而不是替代品发挥作用。”

这项研究被评价为“如何正确使用它”提出了具体方向，而不是对教育领域的生成式人工智能进行无条件监管或盲目采用。

谁是这个故事的幕后黑手？

萨迪·哈雷

生命科学与生态学理学士。 微生物学实验室背景，具有石油、天然气和可再生行业的制药新闻经验。 完整档案 →

安德鲁·齐宁

物理学硕士，具有研究经验。 长期科学新闻爱好者。 在 Science X 的编辑成功中发挥着关键作用。 完整档案 →
